title: Hot tub filterwell dispersant technology
abstract: In some embodiments, the invention provides an apparatus configured to deliver dispersant into the water in a filterwell of a hot tub. Methods for delivering dispersant into the water in a filterwell of a hot tub are also provided.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of delivering a dispersant into water within a filterwell of a hot tub, the method comprising providing a cartridge dispenser containing the dispersant, the cartridge dispenser having a generally cylindrical configuration, and the cartridge dispenser including one or more ports configured to place water within the filterwell in fluid communication with the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser, wherein the method includes placing the cartridge dispenser in a cartridge jacket such that a restricted flow passage is formed between an exterior of the cartridge dispenser and an interior of the cartridge jacket. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein a gap width of the restricted flow passage is greater than 1/32 inch and less than 1/8 inch. 3. The method of claim 2 wherein the gap width of the restricted flow passage is about 1/18-1/14 inch. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein the restricted flow passage is an annular passage. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein the cartridge jacket and the cartridge dispenser placed therein are located within a filterwell basket during said delivering the dispersant into the water within the filterwell of the hot tub. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein the filterwell basket is located in a water circulation system of the hot tub, and the method includes starting and stopping operation of a water circulation pump of the water circulation system. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein the restricted flow passage is a circumferential fluid chamber that: (i) surrounds an entire circumference of the exterior of the cartridge dispenser, and (ii) extends substantially entirely from a top region of the cartridge dispenser to a bottom region of the cartridge dispenser. 8. The method of claim 1 wherein the interior of the cartridge jacket tapers from a wider top region to a more narrow bottom region. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein the exterior of the cartridge dispenser tapers from a wider top region to a more narrow bottom region. 10. The method of claim 8 wherein a gap width of the restricted flow passage is greater adjacent a top region of the cartridge dispenser than it is adjacent a bottom region of the cartridge dispenser. 11. The method of claim 1 wherein said delivering the dispersant into water within the filterwell of the hot tub is performed for at least three weeks before the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser is depleted. 12. The method of claim 1 wherein the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser comprises zinc, malic acid, or both. 13. An apparatus for delivering a dispersant into water within a filterwell of a hot tub, the apparatus comprising a cartridge dispenser containing the dispersant, the cartridge dispenser including one or more ports configured to place water within the filterwell of the hot tub in fluid communication with the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser, the apparatus configured to provide a restricted water flow passage alongside the one or more ports of the cartridge dispenser. 14. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein a gap width of the restricted water flow passage is greater than 1/32 inch and less than 1/8 inch. 15. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein the apparatus includes a cartridge jacket, and the apparatus is configured to define the restricted water flow passage between an exterior of the cartridge dispenser and an interior of the cartridge jacket. 16. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein the apparatus includes a cartridge jacket, and the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket, such that the restricted 

 water flow passage is defined between an exterior of the cartridge dispenser and an interior of the cartridge jacket. 17. The apparatus of claim 16 wherein the cartridge jacket and the cartridge dispenser received therein are located within a filterwell basket, and the filterwell basket is located in a water circulation system of the hot tub. 18. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein the cartridge dispenser has a generally cylindrical configuration, and the restricted water flow passage is a circumferential fluid chamber that: (i) surrounds an entire circumference of an exterior of the cartridge dispenser, and (ii) extends substantially entirely from a top region of the cartridge dispenser to a bottom region of the cartridge dispenser. 19. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein the restricted water flow passage is internal to the cartridge dispenser. 20. The apparatus of claim 19 wherein the cartridge dispenser includes a base wall, the one or more ports open through the base wall, and the cartridge dispenser further includes an outer wall, the outer wall being spaced outwardly from the base wall such that the restncted water flow passage is located between the base wall and the outer wall of the cartridge dispenser. 21. The apparatus of claim 20 wherein a single integral body defines both the base wall and the outer wall of the cartridge dispenser, and wherein the base wall and the outer wall of the cartridge dispenser each have a generally cylindrical configuration. 22. The apparatus of claim 21 wherein the restricted water flow passage is a circumferential fluid chamber surrounding an entire circumference of the base wall of the cartridge dispenser. 23. The apparatus of claim 19 wherein the apparatus includes a cartridge jacket, and the cartridge dispenser is configured to be received in the cartridge jacket. 24. The apparatus of claim 19 wherein the cartridge dispenser includes a base wall, the one or more ports open through the base wall, and the cartridge dispenser further includes a shroud, wherein the shroud does not span an entire perimeter of the cartridge dispenser but rather is localized adjacent the one or more ports, the shroud being spaced outwardly from the base wall such that the restricted flow passage is located between the base wall and the shroud of the cartridge dispenser. 25. The apparatus of claim 24 wherein a single integral body defines both the base wall and the shroud. 26. The apparatus of claim 13 wherein the apparatus includes a cartridge jacket, the cartridge jacket has a shroud, the cartridge jacket is configured to receive the cartridge dispenser, and the apparatus is configured to define the restricted water flow passage between the cartridge dispenser and the shroud of the cartridge jacket. 27. The apparatus of claim 26 wherein the shroud is not configured to span an entire perimeter of the cartridge dispenser but rather is localized adjacent the one or more ports of the cartridge dispenser when the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket. 28. The apparatus of claim 27 wherein the cartridge dispenser has a generally cylindrical configuration, the cartridge dispenser and the cartridge jacket include one or more keys and keyways, and the one or more keys and keyways are configured to retain the cartridge dispenser in a predetermined rotational orientation relative to the cartridge jacket when the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket. 29. The apparatus of claim 28 wherein the one or more ports comprise a plurality of spaced-apart ports arranged linearly along a height of the cartridge dispenser, the shroud of the cartridge jacket is elongated vertically, and the one or more keys and key ways are configured such that the shroud of the cartridge jacket is carried 

 alongside the plurality of spaced-apart ports when the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket. 30. A method of delivering a dispersant into water within a filterwell of a hot tub, using an apparatus comprising a cartridge dispenser, the cartridge dispenser containing the dispersant and including one or more ports that are open to the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser, the apparatus bounding both a first restricted water flow passage and an open area water flow passage, the method including flowing the water through the apparatus such that: (i) a first amount of the water flows through the first restricted water flow passage, thereby placing such water into fluid communication with the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser via the one or more ports, and (ii) a second amount of the water flows through the open area water flow passage. 31. The method of claim 30 wherein the first restricted water flow passage and the open area water flow passage form flow paths in parallel, rather than flow paths in series. 32. The method of claim 30 wherein said flowing the water through the apparatus includes the first amount of the water flowing in a generally downward direction through the first restricted water flow passage, and the second amount of the water flows in a generally downward direction through the open area water flow passage. 33. The method of claim 30 wherein the apparatus includes a weir, and said flowing the water through the apparatus includes the water flowing over and/or through the weir and then downwardly into both the first restricted water flow passage and the open area water flow passage. 34. The method of claim 30 wherein the second amount of the water flowing through the open area w ater flow 7 passage moves at a greater velocity 7 than the first amount of the w ater flowing through the first restricted water flow passage. 35. The method of claim 30 wherein said delivering the dispersant into water within the filterwell of the hot tub is performed for at least three weeks before the dispersant contained in the cartridge dispenser is depleted. 36. The method of claim 30 wherein the first restricted water flow passage is an annular passage. 37. The method of claim 30 wherein the first restricted water flow passage has a smaller cross-sectional area than the open area water flow passage. 38. The method of claim 30 wherein the first restricted water flow passage has a gap width of greater than 1/32 inch and less than 1/8 inch. 39. The method of claim 30 wherein the apparatus is located within a filterwell basket. 40. The method of claim 39 wherein a sidewall of the filterwell basket surrounds both the first restricted water flow passage and the open area water flow passage. 41. The method of claim 40 wherein said flowing the water through the apparatus includes: (i) the first amount of the water flowing through a pervious wall of the filterwell basket after flowing through the first restricted water flow passage, and (ii) the second amount of the w ater flowing through a pervious wall of the filterwell basket after flowing through the open area water flow passage. 42. The method of claim 30 wherein the apparatus further includes a cartridge jacket, and the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket such that the first restricted water flow passage is formed between an exterior of the cartridge dispenser and an interior of the cartridge jacket, and wherein the cartridge jacket and the cartridge dispenser received therein are located in the w ater within the filterw ell of the hot tub during said delivering the dispersant into the water within the filterwell of the hot tub. 43. The method of claim 42 wherein the cartridge j acket and the cartridge dispenser received therein are located within a filterwell basket, the filterwell basket is located in a water circulation system of the hot tub, and the method includes starting and stopping operation of an water circulation pump of the water circulation system. 44. The method of claim 30 wherein the apparatus further bounds a second restricted water flow passage, such that during said flowing the water through the apparatus: (i) the first amount of the water flows through the first restricted water flow passage, (ii) the second amount of the water flows through the open area water flow passage, and (iii) a third amount of the water flows through the second restricted water flow passage. 45. The method of claim 44 wherein the second amount of the water flowing through the open area water flow passage moves at a velocity greater than that of the first amount of the water flowing through the first restricted water flow passage and greater than that of the third amount of the water flow ing through the second restricted water flow passage. 46. The method of claim 44 wherein the second amount of the water is greater than a total of both the first amount of the water and the third amount of the w aler. 47. The method of claim 44 wherein the open area water flow passage is located between the first and second restricted water flow passages. 48. The method of claim 44 wherein the first restricted w ater flow' passage is an annular passage, and the second restricted water flow passage is an annular passage. 49. An apparatus for delivering a dispersant into water within a filterwell of a hot tub, the apparatus comprising a cartridge dispenser containing the dispersant, the cartridge dispenser including one or more ports configured to place water w ithin the filterwell of the hot tub in fluid communication with the dispersant contained 

 in the cartridge dispenser, the apparatus configured to bound: (i) an open area water flow passage, and (ii) a first restricted water flow passage alongside the one or more ports. 50. The apparatus of claim 49 wherein the apparatus is configured such that the first restricted water flow passage and the open area water flow passage are flow paths in parallel, rather than flow paths in series. 51. The apparatus of claim 49 wherein the first restricted water flow passage has a gap width of greater than 1/32 inch and less than 1/8 inch. 52. The apparatus of claim 49 wherein the apparatus is configured to be mounted operatively within a filterwell basket. 53. The apparatus of claim 52 wherein the apparatus is configured to be mounted operatively within the filterw ell basket such that a sidew all of the filterwell basket surrounds both the first restricted water flow- passage and the open area water flow passage. 54. The apparatus of claim 49 wherein the apparatus further includes a cartridge jacket, and the cartridge dispenser is received in the cartridge jacket such that the first restricted w ater flow passage is formed betw een an exterior of the cartridge dispenser and an interior of the cartridge jacket. 55. The apparatus of claim 54 wherein the cartridge jacket and the cartridge dispenser received therein are located within a filterwell basket, and the filterwell basket is located in a water circulation system of the hot tub that includes a water circulation pump. 56. The apparatus of claim 49 wherein the apparatus is configured to further bound a second restricted water flow passage. 57. The apparatus of claim 56 wherein the open area water flow passage is located between the first and second restricted water flow passages. 58. The method of claim 56 wherein the first restricted water flow passage is an annular passage, and the second restricted water flow passage is an annular passage.
